name: canvas_add_widget
description:
Spawn a pre-built widget on the canvas dashboard grid. Fetch the data FIRST with the matching data tool, then pass the result into config — the widget renders server-side, so config IS the finished content. Choosing widget_type:

• 'clock' — clocks AND timers. config={"mode": "clock"|"stopwatch"|"countdown", "duration_seconds": N, "timezone": "America/New_York"}. A TIMER or COUNTDOWN request ('set a timer for 5 minutes', 'pomodoro') is mode='countdown' with duration_seconds (5 minutes → 300) — NOT a plain clock; it starts automatically with Pause/Reset controls. A STOPWATCH request ('time my run') is mode='stopwatch'. A world/city clock ('time in Tokyo') is mode='clock' with an IANA timezone. Multiple timers/clocks can coexist — spawn one widget each.

• 'stock_card' — ANY stock, share price, crypto or ticker ('chart AAPL', 'how is TSLA doing', 'bitcoin price'). Renders the price header, range tabs, chart, technicals and fundamentals together. Call html_notes_stock_history(symbol) first, then pass config={"symbol": "AAPL"} — just the symbol; the server fills in everything else. Do NOT use 'chart' for a ticker: 'chart' has no price header, no technicals and no fundamentals, and is the wrong widget for a stock.
• 'weather' — ANY current weather or forecast ('weather in Tokyo', 'what's it like in London', 'forecast for Denver'). Call html_notes_get_weather(location) first, then pass config={"location": "Tokyo"} — JUST the location; the server fills in the current conditions and 5-day forecast. Do NOT render weather as a data_card and do NOT web-search for it.
• 'scoreboard' — ANY sports scores, fixtures, results or standings. Call html_notes_sports_scores(league) first, then pass config={"league": "nba"} — just the league. Do NOT render scores as a data_card; a scoreboard has to show who is playing whom.
• 'data_card' — the default for fetched text data: news, recipes, search results, weather, facts, lists. Every item needs a 'description' with the actual text; a title+url alone renders as a bare link the user has to click, which is a failure.
• 'chart' — generic numbers only (line/bar/pie from labels+values): populations, poll results, revenue by quarter. NOT for tickers — use 'stock_card'.
• 'image' — display picture URLs. 'youtube_player' — call html_notes_youtube_search first and pass the chosen video_id.
• 'map' — ANY 'where is/are', locations, or map request ('where are the fires in California', 'map of recent earthquakes'). Pass config={"map_query": "<the question>"} — JUST the query; the server web-searches, geocodes the places and drops the markers. Do NOT type coordinates and do NOT render locations as a data_card. For a live-TRAFFIC map, also pass "traffic": true — the server overlays live traffic-flow tiles (green flowing / red jammed).
• 'checklist', 'clock', 'notes', 'iframe_app', 'mini_music_player' — utility widgets, no data fetch needed.

EDITING: to change an existing widget (retime a timer, restyle a clock's timezone, swap a stock, refresh a chart) call this AGAIN with the SAME widget_id and the full updated config — it re-renders that widget in place instead of adding a duplicate. This is the correct way to edit a clock/timer/countdown; do NOT use canvas_modify_dom on these server-rendered widgets, it cannot rebuild them.

REUSE, DON'T DUPLICATE: a map or weather widget is a singleton — there should be at most ONE on the canvas. Before spawning one, check the current canvas (call canvas_read_dom if unsure); if a map/weather is already there, reuse ITS widget_id so the follow-up (another place, a refreshed forecast) UPDATES it in place instead of stacking a second one. Only give a brand-new widget a brand-new id.

widget_type.description: The pre-built widget type to spawn

widget_id.description: A unique ID for the new widget, e.g. 'clock-1' or 'yt-lofi'. To EDIT an existing widget, reuse ITS id from the current canvas — passing an id that is already on screen re-renders that widget in place with the new config instead of adding a duplicate.

config.description: Widget configuration, by widget_type. stock_card: {symbol} — ONLY the symbol, e.g. {"symbol": "AAPL"}; do not copy labels/values/technicals/fundamentals back, the server already has them from html_notes_stock_history. scoreboard: {league} — ONLY the league, e.g. {"league": "nba"}. data_card: {title, subtitle?, icon?, image?, answer?, items:[{title, description, url?, image?, badge?, meta?}]} — use items for lists (news headlines, recipes, results), 'answer' for prose ('content' is accepted as an alias); BETTER for research: pass ONLY a server-side query key and let the server build the card — {'search_query': '<the query>'} for facts/how-tos/comparisons/product picks (attaches each source WITH ITS PHOTO), {'news_topic': '<topic>'} ONLY for current-events you researched with html_notes_news, {'stock_news_query': '<query>'} for market news; 'description' carries the actual text of each item and is REQUIRED — copy the snippet from the search result into it, and if a snippet is empty call html_notes_read_page on that url to get real text. A data_card whose items have only a title and url renders as a naked hyperlink and is useless. chart: {title, type:'line'|'bar'|'pie', labels:[str], values:[num]} or {title, chart:<full Chart.js config>}. image: {title, url, caption?} or {title, images:[{url, caption?}]}. clock: {timezone}. checklist: {title, items:[str]}. notes: {title, content}. iframe_app: {url, title}. youtube_player: {video_id, title}. mini_music_player: {genre, autoplay}. map: {map_query} — ONLY the query string, e.g. {"map_query": "fires in California"}; the server searches, geocodes and drops the markers. weather: {location} — ONLY the location, e.g. {"location": "Tokyo"}; the server already has the conditions from html_notes_get_weather.

name: canvas_modify_dom
description: Modify or REMOVE a widget on the live canvas. ALWAYS target a widget by its exact id selector ('#<widget_id>') taken from canvas_read_dom or the CURRENT CANVAS listing — never by class, tag or position. Class/positional selectors ('.widget-container:first-child', 'h3') match whichever widget happens to come first and will silently destroy the wrong one; a remove that matches more than one widget is now REJECTED and returns the candidate ids so you can retry with the right one. To remove the widget a user described in words ('close the san jose to sf map'), call canvas_read_dom first and match their words against each widget's title and type, then remove that widget's id. Note a directions/route map renders as type 'iframe_app' and a place map as 'map' — both are 'the map' to a user, so match on the TITLE, not the type alone. Do NOT use this to edit server-rendered widgets (clock, weather, stock, map, data_card): call canvas_add_widget again with the SAME widget_id instead.

css_selector.description: CSS selector for the target. Use the widget's exact id selector, e.g. '#traffic-c20bc01b'. Ids come from canvas_read_dom / the CURRENT CANVAS list. Avoid class, tag and positional selectors — they are ambiguous and a remove matching several widgets is rejected.

action.description: The DOM operation: append/prepend add inside the element, replace swaps it, remove deletes it, insert_before/after add as siblings.

html_snippet.description: The HTML to insert. Not required for 'remove' action.

name: canvas_read_dom
description: Read the current live canvas DOM and return a structured summary of all visible widgets — each with its widget_id, type, and what it shows. Call this BEFORE adding a widget that should be a singleton (map, weather) or before editing one: if a widget of that kind is already on the canvas, reuse its widget_id in canvas_add_widget to update it in place instead of spawning a duplicate. Returns element count, component types, text summaries, and optionally the HTML of a specific element matched by CSS selector.

css_selector.description: Optional CSS selector to read a specific element. If omitted, returns full canvas summary.

name: create_widget
description: Create a new visual widget on the canvas dashboard grid. Standard widget types: checklist, clock, notes, iframe_app, mini_music_player, youtube_player, custom.

htmlContent.description: Raw HTML for the widget interface

cssContent.description: Widget-specific styling. Will be isolated to the widget container.

jsContent.description: Widget-specific scripting. Avoid globally scoped assignments.

renderTarget.description: CSS selector to append the widget to, e.g. #dashboard-grid

name: html_notes_create_note
description: Create a new note in the HTML Notes database with a title, sanitized HTML fragment, tags, and optional backlinks.

rendered_html.description: HTML note content using approved subset tags: article, section, p, ul, ol, li, blockquote, hr, strong, em, code, pre, a

tags.description: List of tags to categorize the note

links.description: Optional list of note IDs to link to

name: html_notes_get_note
description: Retrieve the details and full HTML content of a specific note by its ID.

name: html_notes_get_weather
description: Current conditions plus a 5-day forecast for any city or place, via a keyless weather source (Open-Meteo). Sub-second. Returns location, current {temp, feels_like, humidity, wind, condition} and daily {day, hi, lo, condition}. Then call canvas_add_widget(widget_type='weather', config={"location": "<place>"}) — config is JUST the location; the server fills the widget in. Do NOT copy the returned data into the config, do NOT render weather as a data_card, and never web-search for weather.

location.description: City or place name, e.g. 'Tokyo', 'San Francisco', 'Paris, France'

name: html_notes_link_notes
description: Establish a hyperlink connection between a source note and a target note.

target_note_id.description: ID of the target note to connect to

name: html_notes_news
description: Get current news as a ready-to-render data_card of stories, each with a real photo, a tightened headline and a written 2-3 sentence summary. Sourced from live news feeds (GDELT / Google News), so it returns TODAY'S stories with images — unlike html_notes_web_search, which returns news-site homepages and no photos. Use THIS for any 'news', 'headlines', 'latest on X', or 'top stories' request. Pass topic='' for general top stories, or a subject like topic='artificial intelligence'. Then render it with canvas_add_widget(widget_type='data_card', config={'news_topic': '<the same topic>'}) — the server rehydrates the stories, so do NOT re-type the items.

topic.description: News subject, e.g. 'artificial intelligence', 'ukraine', 'nba'. Use an empty string '' for general top stories.

name: html_notes_read_page
description: Fetch the readable text of a web page by URL. Use after html_notes_web_search when a result's snippet is not enough to answer the question.

max_chars.description: Truncate the page text to this many characters (default 6000)

name: html_notes_search_notes
description: Search notes in the HTML Notes database matching a specific query term.

name: html_notes_sports_scores
description: Live scores, fixtures and results for any major league — soccer (FIFA World Cup, Premier League, Champions League, La Liga, MLS), UFC/MMA, NBA, NFL, MLB, NHL, college. Sub-second. Then call canvas_add_widget(widget_type='scoreboard', config={"league": "<league>"}) — config is JUST the league. Do NOT copy the returned events into the config; the server fills them in. Never web-search for scores and never render them as a data_card.

league.description: Friendly league name: fifa, world cup, premier league, champions league, la liga, serie a, bundesliga, mls, soccer, ufc, mma, nba, wnba, nfl, mlb, nhl, college football, college basketball

name: html_notes_stock_history
description: Full snapshot for a stock/ETF/crypto ticker (NVDA, AAPL, BTC-USD): price series plus technicals (RSI, SMA 20/50/200, volatility, 52-week range, trend) and fundamentals (market cap, P/E, EPS, beta, dividend yield, revenue, margin, analyst target, sector). Sub-second. Then call canvas_add_widget(widget_type='stock_card', config={"symbol": "<SYM>"}) — config is JUST the symbol. Do NOT copy the returned data into the config; the server already has it and fills the widget in. Re-typing it wastes about a minute per turn. Never use widget_type='chart' for a ticker, and never web-search for prices.

name: html_notes_stock_news
description: Stock/company/market news headlines plus ticker discovery for a free-text query (a ticker, company name, or topic like 'AI chip stocks'). Returns 'news' (title, publisher, date, url, related tickers) and 'matches' (ticker symbols with names) — use 'matches' to find symbols when the user says 'find me stocks' without naming one, then feed each symbol into html_notes_stock_history for the stock_card. Use THIS for any stock/market news; do NOT use html_notes_stock_history for news (it returns prices only) and do NOT use html_notes_web_search for stock news (this returns cleaner structured headlines). To SHOW the news, call canvas_add_widget(widget_type='data_card', config={'stock_news_query': '<the same query>'}) — the server re-pulls the stories, reads the top articles and writes a 2-3 sentence summary per story; do NOT hand-build items from the raw title+link rows (they have no summaries).

query.description: Ticker, company name, or market topic, e.g. 'NVDA', 'Apple', 'semiconductor stocks'

name: html_notes_update_note
description: Update the content, title, tags, or links of an existing note in the HTML Notes database.

note_id.description: The unique note ID to update (e.g., 'note_123')

links.description: Optional updated list of target link note IDs

name: html_notes_web_search
description: Search the web and return real, current results as [{title, url, snippet}]. This is the ONLY working web search tool — use it for any factual, historical, news, recipe, or 'what/when/who/which is' question before answering. Takes 20-60s to return; that is normal, wait for it.

query.description: Plain-language search query, e.g. 'oldest cannabis video on youtube'

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default 6)

name: html_notes_youtube_search
description: Search YouTube for videos matching a query. Returns a list of results with video_id, title, and channel. Use this FIRST before adding a youtube_player widget, then pass the chosen video_id to canvas_add_widget.

query.description: YouTube search query, e.g. 'lofi hip hop radio' or 'latest video from <channel name>'

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default 5)

order.description: 'relevance' (default), 'date' for newest uploads first, or 'live' to find an actual LIVE STREAM. Use order='live' for any 'X live' / 'live news' / 'watch X live' request — a plain search returns recorded clips, not the stream.

name: list_widget_types
description: Retrieve a list of canonical widget types currently supported by the template registry, along with their parameters schema templates.

name: plan_widget
description: Propose a structured design plan for creating or updating a widget on the canvas dashboard grid. Must be called and approved before create_widget can be executed.

description.description: A short summary of how the widget will behave and style

name: update_widget
description: Update/Patch an existing visual widget on the canvas dashboard grid using its unique widget ID.

name: validate_widget_html
description: Validate a widget's HTML/CSS/JS content before committing it, ensuring HTML tags are closed, CSS rules are safely scoped, and JS has no syntax errors.

name: news_search
description: Current news stories for a topic, with REAL publisher URLs and REAL article photos. Rotates across keyed news providers (gnews, worldnewsapi, currentsapi, thenewsapi, newsapi) and returns the first usable result, so no single provider's daily limit blocks a request. Prefer this over a generic web search for anything time-sensitive: web search returns news-site homepages rather than stories, and Google News RSS returns opaque redirect links with no article photo. Each item carries {title, url, image, source, snippet, date}; `url` is the publisher's own page and `image` is that story's photo, so both are safe to show and to cite. An empty items list means no provider had a usable story, not an error.

topic.description: What to find stories about, e.g. 'James Webb space telescope' or 'federal reserve rate decision'. Multi-word topics are matched as a phrase.

limit.description: How many stories to return (default 6). Some free tiers cap lower and will return fewer.

name: add_to_watchlist
description: Add a stock ticker (e.g., from the Discovery list) to the user's active watchlist. Do NOT use this tool to add items TO discovery; discovery is automatic. Requires user confirmation.

ticker.description: The stock ticker symbol to add (e.g., AAPL).

name: buy_stock
description: Execute a buy order for a stock ticker. Requires user confirmation.

size_pct.description: The percentage of available cash to use (e.g., 0.10 for 10%, 1.0 for 100%). Default is 0.10.

name: calculate_position_size
description: Calculate the number of shares to buy based on risk management rules. Uses the fixed-risk method: risk_amount = cash * risk_percent, shares = risk_amount / (entry_price - stop_loss_price).

risk_percent.description: Max percentage of cash to risk on this trade (e.g., 0.02 for 2%)

name: calculate_risk_reward
description: Calculate the risk-to-reward ratio for a trade setup. A ratio >= 2.0 is generally considered favorable.

stop_loss_price.description: Stop-loss price for the trade (alias: stop_loss)

name: calculate_stop_loss
description: Calculate a stop-loss price based on ATR (Average True Range) volatility. stop_loss = entry_price - (ATR * multiplier).

name: cancel_scheduled_research
description: Cancel a bot-created research schedule (sch-bot-* id from list_scheduled_research) that is no longer worth running — e.g. the thesis changed or the event passed. Frees a slot under the 5-active cap.

reason.description: Why it is no longer needed.

name: clear_watch
description: Deactivate a Watch Desk watch by watch_id, or all active watches for a ticker — use when the thesis changed and the old triggers no longer apply.

ticker.description: Or clear all active watches for this ticker.

name: escalate_to_pm
description: Immediately escalate the cycle to the Board of Directors and Portfolio Manager, bypass standard research loops, and submit the reasoning/findings.

reason.description: The critical reason or justification for escalation.

name: get_congress_trades
description: Fetch recent congressional trading activity for a ticker.

name: get_earnings_data
description: Fetch upcoming or recent earnings dates and estimates for a ticker.

name: get_finnhub_news
description: Get the latest news articles for a stock from Finnhub.

name: get_finviz_fundamentals
description: Fetch fundamental data from Finviz.

name: get_insider_trades
description: Fetch recent insider trading activity for a ticker.

name: get_institutional_holdings
description: Get institutional hedge fund ownership data for a stock. Shows which top hedge funds hold it, position sizes, new positions, quarterly momentum, and whether top-performing funds are invested.

name: get_market_data
description: Get recent price history, fundamentals, financials, and balance sheet for a stock from multiple reliable sources.

name: get_market_map_data
description: Fetches the top movers and market map data for S&P 500 components, grouped by sector. Use this to analyze broad market trends and sector performance for the day.

name: get_options_flow
description: Fetch unusual options activity and flow for a ticker.

name: get_polygon_price_history
description: Fetch historical OHLCV price data from Polygon.

name: get_portfolio_state
description: Get the bot's full portfolio state including cash balance, all open positions with P&L, sector breakdown, and position count. Use this to understand portfolio health and make informed BUY/SELL decisions.

name: get_position_pnl
description: Check the P&L and health of a specific held position. Returns entry price, current price, unrealized P&L, holding duration, and stop-loss proximity. Use this during debates to evaluate whether to hold or sell.

name: get_reddit_trending_stocks
description: Scan the retail-trading subreddits (WSB, r/stocks, r/options, ...) and return the most-mentioned stock tickers right now, ranked by weighted mention count, each with a bull/bear sentiment read and its top post. Use for a market-wide 'what is retail talking about' pulse or to surface candidate tickers. Tickers are validated against yfinance so junk like 'YOLO'/'CEO' is filtered out. This makes many outbound requests and can take 30-120s; keep 'per_subreddit' modest.

limit.description: Max number of ranked tickers to return (1-50). Default 50.

subreddits.description: Optional subreddit names (without 'r/') to scan. Defaults to the 9 main retail-trading subreddits.

per_subreddit.description: How many 'rising' posts to pull per subreddit (1-25). Default 8.

name: get_sec_filings
description: Collect SEC institutional (13F) holdings data for a stock ticker — which top funds hold it and how positions changed. Executes on trading-service; takes ONLY a ticker symbol.

name: get_smart_money_leaderboard
description: Get the best-performing members of Congress or hedge funds ranked by real risk-adjusted performance (alpha vs SPY), with sample sizes. Use to judge how much weight to give a particular actor's trades.

name: get_smart_money_leads
description: Find stocks that multiple members of Congress and/or multiple hedge funds have recently bought - ranked research leads based on consensus buying by informed money. Use to discover candidate tickers when you have no specific ticker in mind.

source.description: Which cohort to mine. Default both.

name: get_smart_money_signal
description: Get smart-money activity for a specific stock: which members of Congress and which hedge funds bought or sold it, when it became public, and how well those actors have historically performed (real alpha vs SPY). Use to check whether informed money is accumulating or exiting a ticker.

name: get_technical_indicators
description: Get computed technical indicators (RSI, MACD, SMA, Bollinger Bands).

name: get_ticker_summary
description: Get a one-call fundamentals snapshot for a ticker: name, sector/industry, market cap, P/E and forward P/E, 52-week range, average volume, dividend yield, analyst price target, and next earnings date.

name: get_upcoming_events
description: Get upcoming scheduled events for a ticker over the next 90 days — currently earnings dates with report timing (bmo = before market open, amc = after market close) and EPS/revenue estimates. Use this to time-snipe research: e.g. earnings on 2026-07-21 amc → schedule_research with when='2026-07-21T21:30:00Z' so the analysis runs on the fresh numbers, not before them.

name: lazy_web_search
description: Search the web for information using a controlled, truncated search engine.

name: list_scheduled_research
description: List all pending/scheduled bot research (active schedules + queued immediate research) plus tickers researched in the last 48h and the governor's limits. ALWAYS check this before requesting or scheduling research — duplicates are rejected.

name: list_watches
description: List active Watch Desk watches (optionally for one ticker) — their triggers, fire counts, and expiry. Check before setting a new watch.

name: read_user_notes
description: Read markdown notes written by the human user. These notes contain human thoughts, context, watchlists, or market observations. Always check this if the user tells you they 'wrote it down' or if you need extra context.

filename.description: Optional. The specific .md filename to read. If omitted, returns a list of all available notes.

name: remove_from_watchlist
description: Remove a stock ticker from the user's active watchlist. Requires user confirmation.

ticker.description: The stock ticker symbol to remove (e.g., AAPL).

name: request_peer_analysis
description: Request a specific peer agent (e.g. 'quant_analyst', 'fundamental_analyst', 'junior_analyst') to run a query, check a lead, or verify details, and post findings to the whiteboard.

target_agent.description: The exact name of the target agent (junior_analyst, fundamental_analyst, quant_analyst).

query.description: The specific question or instructions for the target agent.

name: request_research_now
description: Queue an immediate research-only cycle (collect + analyze, NEVER trades) on up to 5 tickers. Use ONLY when a catalyst already hit and the current data is insufficient. Governor enforces: max 2 queued at once, 4h per-ticker cooldown, dedupe against scheduled research. Be picky — routine curiosity will be rejected; check list_scheduled_research first.

reason.description: The specific catalyst/question this research must answer.

urgency.description: critical bypasses the recency cooldown — reserve for genuine breaking catalysts.

name: run_backtest
description: Backtest a named equation from the library over historical price data. Returns performance metrics: PnL, win rate, max drawdown, Sharpe ratio, and trade list.

equation_name.description: Name of the equation from the library to backtest.

parameters.description: JSON string of parameters for the backtest.

name: run_equation
description: Execute a mathematical equation against live price/technical data for a ticker. You can run a named equation from the library OR provide raw Python code. The code receives 'df' (DataFrame with OHLCV + technicals) and 'params' (dict). Must assign output to 'result'.

ticker.description: Stock ticker to run against (e.g., AAPL).

equation_name.description: Name of a saved equation. If empty, 'code' must be provided.

code.description: Raw Python code if not using a named equation.

name: run_tool_chain
description: Run a named, pre-defined sequence of tools in ONE call instead of calling each tool separately (saves turns/tokens). Pass 'chain' (the chain name) and 'params' (its inputs, e.g. {"ticker": "NVDA"}). Omit 'chain' or pass an unknown name to get the catalog of available chains and their params. Available: ticker_deep_dive (market data + indicators + news + fundamentals for a ticker), chart_with_context (chart + market data + news; params ticker, overlays), news_and_fundamentals (news + fundamentals for a ticker), morning_market_pulse (Reddit-trending tickers + market map; no params). Each underlying tool is also callable on its own.

chain.description: Name of the chain to run. Omit to list available chains.

params.description: Inputs for the chain (e.g. {"ticker": "AAPL"}). See each chain's params.

name: save_equation
description: Save a new mathematical equation to the shared Quant Equation Library. The equation must be Python code that receives 'df' (price DataFrame) and 'params' (dict), and assigns its output to 'result'. Future agents can reuse this equation.

name.description: Unique snake_case name for the equation.

code.description: Python code. Must assign output to 'result'. Has access to df, params, np, pd.

description.description: What this equation does and when to use it.

name: save_trading_chart
description: Render and save an HTML chart and JSON overlay data from the technical analyst agent.

overlays.description: JSON overlays array from the agent

period.description: Timeframe of historical data to fetch (e.g., '1mo', '3mo', '6mo', '1y')

name: schedule_research
description: Schedule a ONE-SHOT research cycle (collect + analyze, NEVER trades) sniped to a specific DATED event. Use this ONLY for a known calendar catalyst (earnings, a Fed decision). For ongoing 'keep an eye on this ticker' monitoring, DO NOT schedule — use watch_ticker instead (it watches price/news/etc. in cheap background code and wakes a cycle only when a condition trips). `when`: OMIT it to auto-snipe the ticker's next earnings (single ticker; the governor resolves the real date/time for you), OR pass an exact ISO-8601 UTC datetime (e.g. '2026-07-21T21:30:00Z') for a non-earnings event. Fires ONCE then auto-deactivates. Coarse market windows and 'monitor' are rejected — use watch_ticker. Governor enforces max 5 active, 10/day, per-ticker dedupe, 4h cooldown; a vague reason is rejected.

tickers.description: 1-5 tickers. Omit `when` only for a SINGLE ticker (earnings auto-snipe).

when.description: Omit to auto-snipe next earnings (single ticker), or an ISO-8601 UTC datetime for a dated event.

reason.description: The specific event/catalyst being sniped and the question to answer (≥10 chars).

review_intent.description: Why this research exists. Default event_followup. (For monitoring, use watch_ticker.)

name: scrape_url
description: Scrape the main text content from a URL. Use this to read articles or SEC filings.

name: search_equations
description: Search the shared Quant Equation Library for existing mathematical strategies. Returns equations with their win rates, Sharpe ratios, and code. Use this before writing a new equation to check if one already exists.

query.description: Search keyword (e.g., 'zscore', 'momentum', 'rsi'). Empty returns top equations by win rate.

name: sell_stock
description: Execute a sell order to close a position for a stock ticker. Requires user confirmation.

ticker.description: The stock ticker symbol to sell (e.g., AAPL).

qty_pct.description: The percentage of the position to sell (e.g., 1.0 for 100%, 0.5 for 50%). Default is 1.0.

name: watch_ticker
description: Leave a 'wake me if…' note on a ticker so the expensive agent stays OFF until a real, thesis-relevant condition trips — the Watch Desk checks it in code (no LLM) and only then re-runs a targeted cycle. Call this at the END of analysing a ticker with the conditions that would change your view. Supersedes any existing watch for the ticker (re-arm). Trigger types: price_above{level}, price_below{level} (target / invalidation levels); pct_change{ref,pct,direction} (moved pct off a reference price; direction up|down|any); rsi{op,value} (op gt|lt); volume_spike{mult} (× 20d avg); news{categories} (earnings|guidance|downgrade|upgrade|mna|litigation|insider); staleness{max_days} (re-check backstop). Always include an invalidation level and a staleness backstop.

thesis_summary.description: Optional 1-2 line current thesis to seed the wake.

expiry_days.description: Auto-expire the watch after N days. Default 30.

name: whiteboard_annotate
description: Add a note or comment to an existing whiteboard entry without overwriting it. Use this to highlight risks, disagree with a consensus, or add context.

entry_id.description: The exact entry_id (from whiteboard_read) to annotate.

author.description: YOUR agent name (e.g. v3_quant_analyst) so teammates know who wrote this. Always provide it.

name: whiteboard_read
description: Read a specific section of the team's shared whiteboard for a given ticker. The whiteboard is a living document where agents collaborate. Sections include: 'market_context', 'risk_flags', 'signals', 'consensus', 'trade_plan'.

ticker.description: The stock ticker to read from.

name: whiteboard_summarize
description: Get a full summary of all sections currently on the whiteboard. Provides a snapshot of the entire team's consensus and state.

name: whiteboard_write
description: Write or overwrite a section of the team's shared whiteboard. Use this to post your final analysis or consensus for other agents to read. Writing will bump the version number of the section. Content should be valid JSON string.

ticker.description: The stock ticker this relates to.

section.description: The section name to write to (e.g. 'consensus').

content.description: The content to write (preferably a JSON string or clear text).

author.description: YOUR agent name (e.g. v3_quant_analyst) so teammates know who wrote this. Always provide it.

name: strain_detail
description: Get full metadata for one strain: breeder, type, description, lineage/parents, cannabinoids, terpenes and genetic neighbors. Does NOT include forum posts or images by default — use strain_forum_posts and strain_images for those.

strain_name.description: Canonical strain name, as returned by strain_search.

include_observations.description: Inline the forum observations too. Default false — they are large.

name: strain_forum_posts
description: Get community forum posts and grow-journal excerpts about a strain, collected from Overgrow, Rollitup, THCFarmer, ICMag and Reddit. Each post has its source, author, URL and text. Use this for real grower experience: yield, difficulty, flowering time, taste.

strain_name.description: Canonical strain name, as returned by strain_search.

source.description: Optional filter by forum: overgrow, rollitup, thcfarmer, icmag or reddit.

name: strain_images
description: Get URLs of photographs of a strain — bud shots and grow photos harvested from forum posts and filtered by a vision model down to actual plant imagery. Returns the image URL plus the post it came from.

strain_name.description: Canonical strain name, as returned by strain_search.

name: strain_import
description: Start a background import of a strain that is not yet in the database. Scrapes SeedFinder for lineage, Kannapedia and Leafly for chemistry, and four forums plus Reddit for posts and photos. Returns immediately with a job_id — the import takes SEVERAL MINUTES. Poll strain_import_status with that job_id until status is 'done', then read the data with the other strain_* tools. Only call this after strain_search has come back empty.

strain_name.description: Name of the strain to import, e.g. 'Blue Dream'.

force.description: Re-scrape and replace existing forum observations. Default false.

name: strain_import_status
description: Check an import started by strain_import. Returns status (queued, running, done or failed), percent complete, a human-readable message, how many posts and images have been saved, and — once done — the imported strain's detail.

name: strain_neighbors
description: Get the k nearest strains to a given strain by genetic and chemical distance, with their distances. Use this to answer 'what is similar to X?'.

strain_name.description: Canonical strain name, as returned by strain_search.

name: strain_search
description: Search the cannabis strain database by name. Returns matching strains with breeder, type, whether a genomic/chemical sample exists, and dominant terpenes. Use this FIRST to resolve a user's fuzzy strain name into a canonical name before calling any other strain_* tool. If it returns no results, the strain is not in the database yet — use strain_import to fetch it.

query.description: Strain name or partial name, e.g. 'blue dream'.

complete_only.description: Only strains that have a complete genomic/chemical sample. Default false.

name: strain_terpene_profile
description: Get the terpene profile for a strain: each terpene with its value, plus the dominant terpene. Terpenes drive a strain's aroma and effect (myrcene = sedating/earthy, limonene = citrus/uplifting, caryophyllene = peppery). Returns an error if no chemical assay exists for the strain.

strain_name.description: Canonical strain name, as returned by strain_search.
